description: Run a proxy mainline TopK anchor at K=128 using Adam and a reduced LR, keeping architecture and sparsity fixed so the round cleanly tests whether optimizer retuning materially lowers FVU.
self_review: This is still a local optimization pass, so the gain may be limited if the quality ceiling is architectural rather than optimizer-driven. I am assuming the default 8e-4 is better suited to signum than Adam.

description: Retry the same K=128 TopK Adam anchor after a narrow startup fix in `sparsify/__main__.py` that skips unnecessary rank-by-rank artifact loading when the model and dataset paths are local.
self_review: This targets startup latency only, so it may not help if the watchdog is being tripped by a different pre-step bottleneck such as dataset shuffling or hook initialization. The quality hypothesis itself remains untested until the run reaches metrics.

description: Retry the same K=128 TopK Adam anchor after a narrow startup fix in `sparsify/__main__.py` that skips unnecessary rank-by-rank artifact loading when the model and dataset paths are local. full
self_review: This targets startup latency only, so it may not help if the watchdog is being tripped by a different pre-step bottleneck such as dataset shuffling or hook initialization. The quality hypothesis itself remains untested until the run reaches metrics.

description: Run a proxy `factorized_topk` experiment at K=128 with the same Adam/LR settings as the current TopK anchor to test whether the added hidden mixing stage can beat the 0.211975 full-baseline trajectory on quality.
self_review: This introduces a new architecture family, so the first proxy result may underperform or show extra compute cost. If it is only marginally better than TopK, it still may not justify promotion given the aggressive goal of roughly halving the current baseline FVU.

description: Run a proxy `mixture_topk` experiment at K=128 with the same Adam/LR settings as the current TopK anchor to test whether soft expert routing can materially beat the current 0.211975 full-baseline trajectory.
self_review: This is another first-pass architecture probe, so it may still be far from competitive or may need family-specific tuning before it shows value. If it is only slightly better than `factorized_topk` but still far behind TopK, that is not enough to justify promotion.

description: Run a proxy `jumprelu` experiment at K=128 with the same Adam/LR settings as the current TopK anchor to test whether learnable thresholding can materially improve reconstruction quality.
self_review: This is still a first-pass family probe, and the default threshold/bandwidth settings may be poorly tuned. If the result is weak, that may mean either the family is unpromising here or that it needs a stabilization pass before a real comparison.

description: Run a proxy `gated` experiment at K=128 with the same Adam/LR settings as the current TopK anchor to test whether decoupling selection and magnitude can materially improve reconstruction quality.
self_review: This is still a first-pass family check, and `gated` may simply inherit the same quality ceiling as plain TopK while adding optimization complexity. If it lands near the other failed prototypes, that strengthens the case for returning to TopK-focused quality work or implementing a genuinely new family.

description: Run a proxy TopK experiment at K=128 with Adam and a modest `ortho_lambda` regularizer to test whether quality can move below the current TopK baseline without changing architecture family.
self_review: This is a local intervention, so the upside may be modest relative to the aggressive target. If it does not beat the existing TopK proxy/full trajectory, the next step should be a more substantial new-family implementation rather than more small TopK tweaks.

description: Run a proxy TopK experiment at K=128 with Adam and a mild matryoshka loss over smaller nested K values to test whether rank-aware training can beat the current TopK baseline.
self_review: This still relies on the current TopK family, so the upside may be limited. If the proxy remains flat or worse, that is a stronger signal that incremental TopK regularization is not enough and the next step should be a more substantial architecture implementation rather than another small training tweak.
